title: China Continues To Be Afforded Luxury Of More Safely Being Able To Carry Out Inflationary Stimulus Measures
section_path: China Continues To Be Afforded Luxury Of More Safely Being Able To Carry Out Inflationary Stimulus Measures
section_path_text: China Continues To Be Afforded Luxury Of More Safely Being Able To Carry Out Inflationary Stimulus Measures
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
China's inflation last month came in at only 0.2%, which is even lower than the 0.3% that was seen in October. Image reference: Chart 1.jpg | assets/2024-12-13_china-continues-to-be-afforded-luxury-of-more-safely-being-able-to-carry-out-inf_img_chart1_d080d3f307d1.jpg
Overall, inflation has remained low in China and has not at all been a debilitating issue as it has been in much of the rest of the world. With inflation remaining relatively low in China, the central government is continuing to be afforded a luxury of more safely being able to carry out inflationary stimulus measures.
